What I'll tell R.

Look, I'll be direct with her. I've been a coordinator for three years, I've delivered consistently, and frankly I've been doing a lot of PM-level work already — running the day-to-day, keeping the programme on track. I think it's time that got recognised with the title and the pay to match.

The way I see it, I've earned the step up. I'm one of the most reliable people on the programme and I'm tired of doing the work without the seniority or the comp that comes with it. Other people I started with have already made PM.

So my ask to R. is straightforward: I want her to put me forward for a PM role, ideally on the next programme. I don't think I need a load of development — I've watched how it's done for three years and I'm confident I'd be good at it. It's really just a matter of giving me the title and letting me run with it.

If there isn't a PM opening here soon, I'll probably start looking externally, because I'm ready now and I don't want to wait around.
